description: Sloth-style SLO monitoring for every service in the mesh. Search a service or instance in the tables at the top, or narrow with the variables. Generated by charts/monitoring-config/templates/prometheusrules/slo-rules.yaml.

panels.title: Search — services and instances under SLO

panels.description: Every service Istio is reporting traffic for, with its live SLO state. Type in a column filter to search; click a header to sort. The variables above narrow this and every panel below.
panels.title: Services

panels.description: The same numbers per workload. A pool can sit comfortably inside its SLO while one instance in it burns budget — the service-level row averages that away, this one does not.
panels.title: Instances

panels.description: Achieved availability over the 30d period, worst of the selected services. Objective: slo.availability.objective in values.yaml.
panels.title: Availability SLI (30d)

panels.description: Share of the 30d budget still unspent. Below 0 means the SLO is already breached for this period.
panels.title: Error budget left (30d)

panels.description: 1x spends the budget exactly over 30d. 6x is the ticket threshold, 14.4x pages — the budget is gone in ~2 days.
panels.title: Current burn rate (5m)

panels.description: The line that matters: a budget trending to zero is a breach you can see coming.
panels.title: Error budget remaining over time

panels.description: The same windows the burn-rate alerts use. Dashed lines are the ticket (6x) and page (14.4x) thresholds.

targets.expr: max by (destination_canonical_service) (slo:sli_error:ratio_rate5m{sloth_slo="availability", sloth_level="service", destination_workload_namespace=~"$namespace", destination_canonical_service=~"$service"}) / max by (destination_canonical_service) (slo:error_budget:ratio{sloth_slo="availability", sloth_level="service", destination_workload_namespace=~"$namespace", destination_canonical_service=~"$service"})

max by (destination_canonical_service) (slo:sli_error:ratio_rate1h{sloth_slo="availability", sloth_level="service", destination_workload_namespace=~"$namespace", destination_canonical_service=~"$service"}) / max by (destination_canonical_service) (slo:error_budget:ratio{sloth_slo="availability", sloth_level="service", destination_workload_namespace=~"$namespace", destination_canonical_service=~"$service"})

max by (destination_canonical_service) (slo:sli_error:ratio_rate6h{sloth_slo="availability", sloth_level="service", destination_workload_namespace=~"$namespace", destination_canonical_service=~"$service"}) / max by (destination_canonical_service) (slo:error_budget:ratio{sloth_slo="availability", sloth_level="service", destination_workload_namespace=~"$namespace", destination_canonical_service=~"$service"})

max by (destination_canonical_service) (slo:sli_error:ratio_rate3d{sloth_slo="availability", sloth_level="service", destination_workload_namespace=~"$namespace", destination_canonical_service=~"$service"}) / max by (destination_canonical_service) (slo:error_budget:ratio{sloth_slo="availability", sloth_level="service", destination_workload_namespace=~"$namespace", destination_canonical_service=~"$service"})

panels.title: Burn rate by alert window

panels.description: Raw bad-event share per service — the input every window above is built from.
panels.title: SLI error ratio (5m)
Latency SLO — requests slower than the threshold

panels.description: Achieved latency over the 30d period, worst of the selected services. Objective: slo.latency.objective over slo.latency.thresholdMs.
panels.title: Latency SLI (30d)

panels.description: Share of the 30d budget still unspent. Below 0 means the SLO is already breached for this period.
panels.title: Error budget left (30d)

panels.description: 1x spends the budget exactly over 30d. 6x is the ticket threshold, 14.4x pages — the budget is gone in ~2 days.
panels.title: Current burn rate (5m)

panels.description: The line that matters: a budget trending to zero is a breach you can see coming.
panels.title: Error budget remaining over time

panels.description: The same windows the burn-rate alerts use. Dashed lines are the ticket (6x) and page (14.4x) thresholds.

targets.expr: max by (destination_canonical_service) (slo:sli_error:ratio_rate5m{sloth_slo="latency", sloth_level="service", destination_workload_namespace=~"$namespace", destination_canonical_service=~"$service"}) / max by (destination_canonical_service) (slo:error_budget:ratio{sloth_slo="latency", sloth_level="service", destination_workload_namespace=~"$namespace", destination_canonical_service=~"$service"})

max by (destination_canonical_service) (slo:sli_error:ratio_rate1h{sloth_slo="latency", sloth_level="service", destination_workload_namespace=~"$namespace", destination_canonical_service=~"$service"}) / max by (destination_canonical_service) (slo:error_budget:ratio{sloth_slo="latency", sloth_level="service", destination_workload_namespace=~"$namespace", destination_canonical_service=~"$service"})

max by (destination_canonical_service) (slo:sli_error:ratio_rate6h{sloth_slo="latency", sloth_level="service", destination_workload_namespace=~"$namespace", destination_canonical_service=~"$service"}) / max by (destination_canonical_service) (slo:error_budget:ratio{sloth_slo="latency", sloth_level="service", destination_workload_namespace=~"$namespace", destination_canonical_service=~"$service"})

max by (destination_canonical_service) (slo:sli_error:ratio_rate3d{sloth_slo="latency", sloth_level="service", destination_workload_namespace=~"$namespace", destination_canonical_service=~"$service"}) / max by (destination_canonical_service) (slo:error_budget:ratio{sloth_slo="latency", sloth_level="service", destination_workload_namespace=~"$namespace", destination_canonical_service=~"$service"})

panels.title: Burn rate by alert window

panels.description: Raw bad-event share per service — the input every window above is built from.
panels.title: SLI error ratio (5m)

panels.description: Which pod is actually serving the 5xx. A single bad instance shows up here long before the pool SLO moves.
panels.title: Availability error ratio per instance

panels.description: Share of requests over the latency threshold, per pod.
panels.title: Latency error ratio per instance

panels.description: The distribution behind the latency SLI. The SLI counts threshold breaches; this shows how far past it they land.
panels.title: P95 latency per instance

panels.description: Traffic split across the pool. An instance with a bad SLI and near-zero traffic is a different problem than one under load.
panels.title: Request rate per instance

panels.description: Unaggregated traffic by status code — the ground truth the availability SLI is derived from.
panels.title: Request rate by response code

panels.description: Latency distribution across the selected services.
panels.title: Request duration quantiles

list.description: Namespaces with services currently under SLO.
list.label: Namespace
list.name: namespace

list.description: Type to search. Narrows every panel below.
list.label: Service
list.name: service

title: Service & Instance SLO (Sloth)
